comments.comment: Updated methodology of calculating fugitive emissions of chromium from ore stockpiling and surface crushing operations using more recent EPA emission factors. This resulted in higher emissions calculated from these operations. However, this increase was o

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Please note that the fugitive emissions calculated from ore stockpiling and crushing operations increased due to application of more recent EPA emission factors for these operations. However, there was a net decrease in overall releases as a result of muc

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The total amount of copper emissions increased due the use of more recent EPA emission factors associated with the stockpiling and crushing of ore at our site. Air emissions of copper decreased from 1999 levels as a results of much lower production levels

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increases in lead emissions in 2000 versus 1999 was due to change in methodology for calculating fugitive emissions from ore stockpiling and surface crushing activities. More recent EPA emission factors were used resulting in a higher calculated emission

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Changes in manganese emissions in 2000 were primarily due to a change in methodology of calculating fugitive emissions from ore stockpiling and surface crushing operations. This resulted in the total emissions of manganese being higher in 2000 as compared

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Changes in 2000 emission rate for nickel was primarily due to a change in methodology in calculation of fugitive emissions from surface ore stockpiling and crushing operations. More recent EPA emission factors were used in this submission.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Emissions of selenium in water increased by nearly 20% over 1999 levels. However, this is on the basis of one water sampling result at a very low detection limit. More data is required in the future to define more typical selenium concentrations.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Overall zinc emissions decreased in 2000 by approximately 10% as a result of much lower emissions in water due to a combination of lower effluent discharge volumes and lower zinc concentrations in effluent. Note that the methodology of calculating fugitiv

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increases in ammonia releases to water in 2000 were 50% higher than 1999 primarily due to an increase in ammonia concentration in water.

Concentrations in effluent were significantly lower in 1998 as compared toì

Detected Selenium in quarterly elemental scan in April 1989 at a level of ì
0.006 mg/l. Assumed that average concentration for year was at this levelì

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Zinc concentrations in effluent significantly lower in 1998 as opposed to ì

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Arsenic in tailings not considered as byproduct in previous years. However, ì
arsenic exceeds the 10 mt per year criteria and will be reported.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Tailings discharged to tailings area not considered as byproduct in ì
previous years. However, chromium in tailings greater than 10 mtpyr due to ì
large tonnage of tailings produced and meets reporting criteria.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Cobalt in tailings was not considered as a byproduct until this year. Due ì
to large annual tonnages in tailings, cobalt exceeds the 10 mt per year ì
reporting requirement.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Used higher average for copper in airborne dust from Crushing plant exhaust ì
systems than used in previous years. Previously, only general atmosphere ì
dust monitoring results were used. For 1997, results from dust samplingì
throughout plant were used an

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Tailings not considered as byproduct until this year. Due to large tonnages ì
of tailings, lead exceeds the reporting threshold of 10 mt per year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Manganese not considered as byproduct in tailings until this year. Due to ì
large tonnages of tailings, manganese exceeds the 10 mt per year reporting ì
threshold.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Selenium in tailings not considered as byproduct in previous years. ì
However, selenium exceeds the 10 mt per year reporting threshold due to the ì
large tonnages of tailings produced.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Zinc in tailings not considered as byproduct until this year. Due to largeì
tonnages of tailings, zinc exceeds the 10 mt per year reporting threshold.

Ammonia originates from mine water( i.e. from explosives used underground ì
